summary: Pre-install twining-mcp globally in Docker image instead of using npx at runtime
context: Smoke test on Fly.io failed because npx -y twining-mcp needed to download the package at runtime inside the slim production container. Agents couldn't call twining_assemble or twining_decide.
rationale: Adding RUN npm install -g twining-mcp to the Dockerfile and changing condition configs from npx to direct twining-mcp command ensures the MCP server is always available. Verified by passing smoke test on Fly.io.

alternatives.option: Keep npx but pre-warm the cache during Docker build
alternatives.reason_rejected: npx cache location varies and can be unreliable in slim containers — direct global install is simpler and deterministic
